hard_constraints.description: Never prescribe an exercise or movement family that violates an explicit movement restriction.
Never prescribe an exercise variant that requires unavailable equipment when no approved fallback exists.
Do not plan beyond the user's declared session-time ceiling when deriving minimum generated Full Body planning inputs.
hard_constraints.rationale: Time feasibility is a hard boundary for executable planning.

hard_constraints.description: Do not use any single authored weekly layout as the default target for generated session topology, day-role sequence, movement-pattern distribution, or slot ordering.
hard_constraints.rationale: Generated output must remain original and doctrine-driven.

soft_preferences.description: Honor the user's requested split when it remains feasible after hard-constraint and feasibility checks.
Prefer lower-complexity exercise options when multiple valid options are otherwise tied.
soft_preferences.rationale: Lower complexity generally improves adherence and execution quality.

soft_preferences.description: Prefer generated Full Body when the user does not specify a split for the current generated scope.
soft_preferences.rationale: Generated Full Body is the only active generated split in scope.

soft_preferences.description: Bias toward simpler options for novice and comeback users when valid choices exist.
soft_preferences.rationale: Simplicity improves recoverability and execution quality early on.

soft_preferences.description: Bias toward more recoverable options for low-recovery users when valid choices exist.
soft_preferences.rationale: Recoverability takes priority over theoretical best stimulus under low-recovery conditions.

soft_preferences.description: Bias toward adherence-preserving choices for inconsistent-schedule users when valid choices exist.
soft_preferences.rationale: Sustained adherence matters more than ideality when schedule consistency is unstable.

constraint_resolution_policy.same_tier_tiebreaker: prefer_lower_fatigue_and_lower_complexity_for novice, comeback, low_recovery, technique_limited, and inconsistent_schedule users
constraint_resolution_policy.rationale: Conflicts must resolve deterministically and conservatively.

minimum_viable_program_policy.rationale: If ideal programming is not possible, the system must degrade to a safe and executable minimum viable program.

anti_overadaptation_policy.rationale: The system should not churn the plan faster than the user can produce interpretable evidence.

data_sufficiency_policy.rationale: Major changes require enough data unless safety requires faster intervention.
